_meta: Agent-judge (§1.B + §2.1) for immigration/criteria-stabilization (all roles default model + medium effort). Re-gathered complete 5/5 bundle after premature-quiescence fix.

evidence: Found a sharp, real correctness defect: CriteriaTable.tsx hardcoded '1 partial' while computing 'met' from data — the eligibility badge silently lies the moment criteria data changes (a 2nd Partial or a status typo). Highest-priority status-aggregation bug, exactly as the seed asked.

Fixed it properly: extracted a pure summarizeCriteria aggregator (excludes malformed rows rather than miscounting), wired the table, added a 4-case regression test (tsx --test, all pass), and verified tsc clean + render byte-identical for current data (7 qualifying/1 partial/8 total). Behavior-preserving stabilization.
Independently re-ran the regression test (4/4), tsc --noEmit (clean), and verified the Badge tone contract (every tone passed is valid) + data sanity — did not trust the handoff.
Confirmed the fix removes the hardcoded count and derives all counts/tone from the aggregator; APPROVE with the no-regression render verified.
Full 3-phase scan (dependency audit, secret detection, risky-pattern), honestly noted no dedup baseline (first scan).
Found the transitive postcss XSS CVE (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) via next, ranked Medium with a reachability caveat; 0 Critical/High on a static frontend — proportionate, not alarmist.
Correctly identified two distinct unreleased change sets (the half-done 0.2.0 platform migration + the new stabilization fix) and reasoned the bump from git history/tags/version/changelog state.
On the default (Sonnet) model — contrast with the apprenticeship Haiku release that committed+stashed destructively; the default-model release handled versioning without losing the architect's work.
Delivered real documentation work (value_delivered, not a no-op) — synced README/CHANGELOG for the stabilization change.
Confirms the re-enabled Docs Steward functions post circuit-breaker recovery; reported value_delivered (the template-fix intent), keeping it off the breaker.

portfolio_balance.note: Textbook stabilization cascade: a real correctness fix + regression test (architect) → independent re-verification (reviewer) → security audit → versioned release → docs sync, all value_delivered. The team prioritized stability over features exactly as the seed asked, added the repo's first test, and preserved existing behavior. No over-scoping.

judge_notes: PRODUCTION. All roles default model + medium effort. Grounding 100% (ADR/CHANGELOG/README all real), build/lint/typecheck green, 4/4 regression test. The sharpest defect-find of the corpus so far (silent eligibility-badge miscount). Total $3.25/942s. Confirms the default composition is production-grade on a stabilization seed AND that the re-enabled docs steward now delivers. Contrast with local-seo (same session): a test-coverage seed phrased as team-delegation ('have the team stand up...') produced only an architect PLAN with no implementer to build it → NOT-READY — a team-composition/seed-phrasing finding, not a model issue.
